content:
Hey folks, I'm going crazy for a simple little thing I'm sure there's a way to fix, but I can't Google.
When I cite a theorem, do you write something like:

\begin{theorem}[{\cite{smith2001foo}}]
All foos are bars.
\end{theorem}

Leaving aside the horrible double bracket (if you don't wrap \cite in an additional curly bracket, it complains), the result is meh since the citation (already wrapped in square brackets) gets wrapped in an additional pair of round brackets: Screenshot-from-2020-04-07-22-57-55.png

Do you know another way?
Well, in the code you gave you open \cite with a [ and close it with a }, is this a typo?
